WITH REGARD TO THE 1ST, 2ND AND 7TH CAUSES OF ACTION, THE COURT ACCEPTS THE CITY'S POSITION THAT THEY WILL NOT ARGUE THAT THEY HAVE POWERS TO ELIMINATE ANY DAMAGES THAT FLOW FROM A BREACH OF THE SETTLEMENT AGREEMENT. DEFENDANTS SHALL PREPARE THE PROPOSED ORDER. JUDGE: HAROLD KAHN; CLERK: FELICIA GREEN; COURT REPORTER: CAROL KAREN, CSR NO. 8189, EMAIL: CKAREN@SFTC.ORG

CASE MANAGEMENT CONFERENCE
Off Calendar Apr-12-2019 Continued To Jul-03-19 At 10:30 A.M. In Dept 610

Respondent City And County Of San Francisco Motion For Summary Judgment , And In The Alternative For Summary Adjudication (Added For Purposes Of Entering Tentative Ruling)
Off Calendar Mar-26-2019 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
Respondent City And County Of San Francisco Motion For Summary Judgment , And In The Alternative For Summary Adjudication (Added For Purposes Of Entering Tentative Ruling)

Off Calendar. Duplicate entry for tentative ruling purposes only (302/EPS)

MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT , And In The Alternative For Summary Adjudication
Off Calendar Mar-26-2019 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
Appearances:
Michael F. Wright, Esq., 310-712-6195; James A. Reuben, Esq., Reuben Junius & Rose, 415-567-9000, appearing for Plaintiff;
Jaems M. Emery, Esq, Deputy city Attorney CCSF, 415-554-4628, appearing for Defendant, CCSF;

RULING - A R G U E D. Court having reviewed the pleadings and having heard oral argument rules as follows:

RESPONDENT CITY AND COUNTY OF SAN FRANCISCO'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT, And In The Alternative For Summary Adjudication.

Respondent, Defendant, and Cross-Complainant City and County of San Francisco's motion for summary judgment is granted. The City is entitled to summary judgment on plaintiff's Fourth Amended Complaint and the City's Cross-Complaint. (see word minutes for complete details)

Order is signed.

Judge: Ethan P. Schulman; Clerk: W. Trupek; Court Reporter: Maria Torreano CSR# 8600 email: maria.torreano@gmail.com (302/EPS)

MOTION FOR SUMMARY ADJUDICATION
Off Calendar Mar-26-2019 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
Appearances:
Michael F. Wright, Esq., 310-712-6195; James A. Reuben, Esq., Reuben Junius & Rose, 415-567-9000, appearing for Plaintiff;
Jaems M. Emery, Esq, Deputy city Attorney CCSF, 415-554-4628, appearing for Defendant, CCSF;

RULING - A R G U E D. Court having reviewed the pleadings and having heard oral argument rules as follows:

PLAINTIFF CONTEST PROMOTIONS, LLC, MOTION FOR SUMMARY ADJUDICATION

Contest Promotions, LLC's motion for summary adjudication is off calendar as moot in light of the ruling on the City's motion for summary judgment.

Order is signed.

Judge: Ethan P. Schulman, Clerk: W. Trupek, Court Reporter: Maria Torreano, CSR#8600, maria.torreano@gmail.com, Reported. =(302/EPS)

CASE MANAGEMENT CONFERENCE

CASE MANAGEMENT CONFERENCE
Off Calendar Aug-10-2018 Continued To Jan-30-19 At 10:30 A.M. In Dept 610

CASE MANAGEMENT CONFERENCE
Off Calendar Jun-11-2018 Continued To Aug-29-18 At 10:30 A.M. In Dept 610

DEMURRER TO PETITION FOR WRIT OF MANDATE/ PROHIBITION/ CERTIFICATION
Off Calendar Mar-28-2017 Overruled
MINI MINUTES:
Appearances:
Michael F. Wright, Attorney at Law, Tel. 310.712.6195, for plaintiff Contest Promotions;
James M. Emery, of Office of the City Attorney, Tel. 415.554.4700, for defendants City and County of San Francisco.

Ruling: A R G U E D. The Court modifies its tentative ruling. RESPONDENT CITY AND COUNTY OF SAN FRANCISCO's DEMURRER TO SECOND AMENDED PETITION and COMPLAINT is overruled as to the 4th, 5th and 6th causes of action, and sustained without leave to amend as to the 1st, 2nd, 3rd and 7th causes of action. With regard to the 1st, 2nd and 7th causes of action, the Court accepts the City's position that they will argue that they have powers to eliminate any damages that flow from a breach of the settlement agreement. Defendants shall prepare the proposed order. Judge: Harold Kahn; Clerk: Felicia Green; Court Reporter: Carol Karen, CSR No. 8189, email: ckaren@sftc.org; Dept. 302.

Notice Of Motion And Motion To Consolidate Actions
Off Calendar Jul-28-2016 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
Appearances:
Attorney, Michael F. Wright, Esq. (310-712-6195) appears telephonically as counsel for petitioner/moving party

No other appearance is made in this matter.

The Court confirms the tentative ruling.

PETITIONER CONTEST PROMOTIONS, LLC'S Motion To Consolidate Actions is GRANTED. Defendant City of San Francisco does not oppose the motion. This case is consolidated with case number 547830 for all purposes. This case is the lead case and all further filings must be in this case only. Single written order is signed and filed in open court.

Judge: Harold Kahn; Clerk: Jose Rios Merida; Reporter: Giselle Casey, CSR #8098, Bus. Tel: 415-717-9768. (D302)
